title: अग्नि द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अग्नि द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

प्रथमं अग्निमित्युक्तं द्वितीयं वैश्वानरम्।
तृतीयं जातवेदसं चतुर्थं हव्यवाहनम्॥१॥

पञ्चमं पावकं नाम षष्ठं विभावसुं विदुः।
सप्तमं हुताशनं चैव अष्टमं दहनं तथा॥२॥

नवमं सर्वभक्षं च दशमं सप्तजिह्वकम्।
एकादशं धूमकेतुं द्वादशं हिरण्यरेतसम्॥३॥

द्वादशैतानि नामानि अग्निदेवस्य कीर्तयेत्।
हवनं कुर्वतां नित्यं अग्निसन्तोषकारकम्॥४॥

सर्वयज्ञफलं तस्य रोगनाशश्च जायते।
गृहशान्तिकरं प्रोक्तं अग्निकृपा सदा भवेत्॥५॥

॥ इति श्री अग्नि द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Agni Dwadash Nama Stotram ||

Prathamam Agnim-ityuktam Dvitiyam Vaishvanaram.
Tritiyam Jatavedasam Chaturtham Havya-vahanam.||1||

Panchamam Pavakam Nama Shashtham Vibhavasum Viduh.
Saptamam Hutashanam Chaiva Ashtamam Dahanam Tatha.||2||

Navamam Sarva-bhaksham Cha Dashamam Sapta-jihvakam.
Ekadasham Dhuma-ketum Dvadasham Hiranya-retasam.||3||

Dvadashai-tani Namani Agni-devasya Kirtayet.
Havanam Kurvatam Nityam Agni-santosha-karakam.||4||

Sarva-yajna-phalam Tasya Roga-nashash-cha Jayate.
Griha-shanti-karam Proktam Agni-kripa Sada Bhavet.||5||

|| Iti Shri Agni Dwadash Nama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: प्रथम नाम अग्नि, दूसरा वैश्वानर, तीसरा जातवेदस, चौथा हव्यवाहन। पाँचवाँ पावक, छठा विभावसु, सातवाँ हुताशन, आठवाँ दहन। नवाँ सर्वभक्ष, दसवाँ सप्तजिह्व, ग्यारहवाँ धूमकेतु, बारहवाँ हिरण्यरेतस। अग्निदेव के इन बारह नामों का कीर्तन करने से हवन का पूर्ण फल मिलता है, रोगनाश होता है और गृहशान्ति प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Twelve Vedic names of Lord Agni for ritual recitation
Pleases Agni Deva and enhances the fruits of havan/yajna
Traditionally recited for removal of diseases and purifies the household
Invokes griha shanti (peace in the home)
Essential for those who perform daily agnihotra
description: Dedicated to Lord Agni, the Agni Dwadash Nama Stotram (अग्नि द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Rig Veda, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to grant griha shanti (peace in the home) and please Agni Deva and enhance the fruits of havan/yajna. It is also said to twelve Vedic names of Lord Agni for ritual recitation. It is especially recommended on Sunday (Ravivar) and Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Holi for maximum spiritual benefit. At 5 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 3 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Agni Dwadash Nama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Agni Dwadash Nama Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Agni Dwadash Nama Stotram (अग्नि द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्) is a short 5-verse Sanskrit composition that lists the 12 (dvadasha) most sacred names of Lord Agni — the Vedic god of fire who is the cosmic-mediator (deva-duta) between humans and devas, accepting all offerings (havya) into the yajna-fire and conveying them to the higher gods. The 12 names are: (1) Agni — the leader; (2) Vaishvanara — universal-man; (3) Jatavedas — knower of all that is born; (4) Havyavahana — carrier of offerings; (5) Pavaka — purifier; (6) Vibhavasu — possessor of light; (7) Hutashana — eater of oblations; (8) Dahana — burner; (9) Sarva-bhaksha — all-consuming; (10) Sapta-jihvaka — seven-tongued (the seven flames); (11) Dhuma-ketu — smoke-bannered; (12) Hiranyaretasa — golden-seeded. The text is preserved in the Vedic-Smriti tradition. (Source: Rig Veda / Smriti dvadasha-nama tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Agni's 12 sacred names?
seoFaqs.answer: Each name reveals one cosmic function of Agni: (1) Agni — leading-deity invoked first in every yajna; (2) Vaishvanara — the universal-fire dwelling as digestive jathara-agni in all beings (Bhagavad Gita 15.14); (3) Jatavedas — knower of every being's birth and karma — the cosmic witness; (4) Havya-vahana — vehicle of oblations to the devas; (5) Pavaka — purifier of substances and sins; (6) Vibhavasu — wealth-of-radiance, source of all luminosity; (7) Hutashana — eater of the hutam (offered) substances; (8) Dahana — burner that destroys impurity; (9) Sarva-bhaksha — accepter of all offerings without discrimination; (10) Sapta-jihvaka — seven-tongued referring to the seven sacred flame-types Kali, Karali, Manojava, Sulohita, Sudhumra-varna, Sphulingini, Vishvaruchi (Mundaka Upanishad 1.2.4); (11) Dhuma-ketu — smoke-bannered, since smoke is Agni's standard rising to heaven; (12) Hiranya-retasa — golden-seeded, refers to Agni receiving Shiva's golden-tejas that became Kartikeya. (Sources: Mundaka Upanishad; Bhagavad Gita 15.14; Vedic deity classifications.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Agni the foremost Vedic deity?
seoFaqs.answer: Agni occupies a unique supreme position in Vedic religion because: (1) the Rigveda — the oldest religious text in human history — opens with Agnimile Purohitam Yajnasya Devam Ritvijam (I praise Agni, the priest, the sacrificial god, the invoker) — Mandala 1, Sukta 1; (2) the Rigveda contains 200+ hymns dedicated to Agni — second only to Indra; (3) Agni is invoked first in every yajna (agnyadhana) before any other deity is called — making Him the deva-mukha (mouth of the gods); (4) without Agni accepting and conveying offerings, no yajna can succeed; (5) in cosmology, Agni is one of the three primary forms of Brahman (Agni-Vayu-Surya) representing the earthly, atmospheric, and celestial fire; (6) in the body, Agni is the digestive jathara-agni and the kundalini-agni; (7) at death, Agni-samskara (cremation) returns the body to its source. The 12-name stotra distills all these aspects into a daily-chantable namavali. (Sources: Rigveda Mandala 1; Aitareya Brahmana; Brihadaranyaka Upanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Agni Dwadash Nama be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Agni Dwadash Nama is most powerfully recited on Sunday (Ravivar — fire-day), Tuesday (Mangalvar — Mars/fiery day), and Thursday for daily Vedic-Brahmin worship; before every havan, homa, agnihotra, yajna, or puja that uses fire (deepa, dhupa); during Holi (the Holika-dahan night when Agni purifies); during Diwali Lakshmi puja (when sacred lamps are lit); during fire-related Vedic samskaras like Upanayana (sacred-thread initiation), Vivaha (marriage saptapadi around fire), Antyeshti (cremation), Grihapravesha (housewarming with fire-yajna), and Punyahavachana. Daily-recitation by orthodox Brahmins is part of the morning Sandhya Vandana before lighting the household lamp or kindling the agnihotra fire. The 12-names are also chanted before any Vedic havan-kunda is lit. (Sources: Brahma-yajna paddhati; Agnihotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Agni Dwadash Nama promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 4-5 of the stotra — the phala-shruti — promise three specific benefits: (1) Sarva-yajna-phalam tasya — the reciter receives the full fruit of all yajnas (since invoking the 12 names is itself equivalent to performing all yajnas); (2) Roga-nashash-cha jayate — diseases are destroyed (since Agni is Vaishvanara — the digestive-fire whose proper functioning is the basis of arogya in Ayurveda); (3) Griha-shanti-karam proktam Agni-kripa sada bhavet — peace in the household and constant grace of Agni. Additionally, Agni-kripa is held to grant: tejas (radiance/personal-charisma), success in fire-related professions (cooking, smelting, electricity, technology), purification from sins (since Agni is Pavaka), success of all yajnas and rituals (since Agni is the deva-mukha), protection of the home from fire-accidents and enemy-attacks, and ultimately moksha through the Agni-marg (Devayana — northern path) of Upanishadic liberation. (Sources: phala-shruti verses 4-5; Vedic Agni-stuti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Agni Dwadash Nama related to the daily Agnihotra ritual?
seoFaqs.answer: The Agnihotra is the daily Vedic-Brahmin fire-ritual prescribed in the Yajurveda — performed at sunrise and sunset using sacred grhya-agni in a copper homa-kunda with offerings of cow-ghee, milk, and rice grains. Reciting the Agni Dwadash Nama before lighting the Agnihotra fire is essential because: (1) calling Agni by all 12 names invokes His complete cosmic power into the small ritual fire; (2) it transforms the household lamp/fire into a yajna-agni capable of carrying offerings to the devas; (3) the daily-recitation establishes a personal relationship with Agni — building agnyadhana-shakti (fire-establishment power) over years; (4) it ensures the agnihotra-phala (fruit of fire-ritual) — protection of the household, success of yajnas, and continuation of Vedic dharma. Even Agnihotri Brahmins who cannot perform the full yajna daily are advised to at least recite this 12-name stotra to maintain Agni-kripa. (Sources: Agnihotra Brahmana; Apastamba Grhya Sutra; Vedic ritual paddhati.)
